steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes the order in which the two lighthouse keepers began their careers. The notes show Clark started in 1817 and Ellis started in 1883. The correct choice must make that chronological sequence explicit.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Eleanor M. Clark's career as a lighthouse keeper ended in 1878, whereas Miriam J. Ellis's ended in 1917.’: addresses when their careers ended, not when they began. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Miriam J. Ellis began her career as a lighthouse keeper years after Eleanor M. Clark did.’: directly establishes the start sequence using the data (Clark 1817, Ellis 1883), making clear who came first. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘From 1817 to 1878, the nighttime waters of Connecticut were more navigable thanks to lighthouse keepers Miriam J. Ellis and Eleanor M. Clark.’: incorrectly places Ellis in Connecticut during 1817–1878, which misreads the notes. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Before automation, lighthouse keepers like Eleanor M. Clark and Miriam J. Ellis were crucial to ensuring safe navigation for watercraft.’: makes a general statement about lighthouse keepers' importance without addressing when either career started. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
